exemplar.title: How does Bong Joon-Ho utilise symbolism in his 2019 film Parasite to critique class disparities present in South Korean culture?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Bong Joon-Ho utilise symbolism in his 2019 film Parasite to critique class disparities present in South Korean culture?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Secure factual knowledge of plot, characters and socio-historical context (e.g., âgolden spoon vs. dirt spoonâ).
Sustained link between film details (rock, basements, peaches, smell, rain) and the overarching question of class disparity.
Consistent and relevant use of quotations, time-stamps and reputable secondary sources to support claims.
Conceptual linking of Marxist terminology and East Asian cultural references enriches interpretation.
weaknesses: Interpretation does not probe deeper cinematic intentions (mise-en-scÃ¨ne, editing, tonal shifts).
Occasional lapses into thematic generalities rather than fully original insights.
Selection of references, while appropriate, lacks variety in cinematic techniques or visual frames.
Does not consistently demonstrate the persuasive depth required for the highest band.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 4 is warranted because the essay exhibits good, sustained knowledge and interpretation of symbols in Parasite, supported by well-chosen textual references and secondary evidence. However, it stops short of the nuanced, persuasive cinematic analysis and highly varied textual support characteristic of Band 5.

strengths: Generally appropriate Marxist analysis of symbols (e.g., rock as false consciousness, rain as class suffering).
Insightful interpretation of the rain motif to contrast social classesâ vulnerability.
weaknesses: Analysis is often explanatory or thematic, with limited close reading of filmic techniques (lighting, camera angles, sound).
Some paragraphs drift into plot summary (peach-allergy deception), reducing evaluative focus.
Insightful comments appear in isolation but are not sustained throughout the essay.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 reflects that the essay offers generally appropriate analysis and some evaluative insights, but relies heavily on description and summary, with minimal detailed consideration of cinematic features. To reach Band 4 or higher, close readings of specific filmic choices must be more fully integrated and sustained.

strengths: Clear line of inquiry with each paragraph dedicated to a discrete symbol, enabling logical progression.
Effective use of topic sentences and transitions to maintain cohesion.
Supporting examples (dialogue, timestamps, secondary data) are mostly well embedded in the prose.
weaknesses: Some body paragraphs (peaches, smell) are underdeveloped and could be better balanced.
Minor formatting issues (superscripts, stray symbols) interrupt the flow.
The conclusion restates rather than synthesises insights, weakening the final evaluative impact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 4 is appropriate because the essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with an adequately developed line of inquiry and generally smooth integration of evidence. However, occasional underdevelopment of sections, formatting artefacts and a less effective conclusion prevent it from reaching the top band.

strengths: Language is clear, academically appropriate and largely accurate in grammar and vocabulary.
Formal analytical register is sustained, with correct use of discipline-specific terminology.
Sentence structures are varied, aiding readability.
weaknesses: Minor mechanical errors and formatting artefacts (rogue symbols, inconsistent citation style).
Occasional colloquial expressions (e.g., âdog-eat-dog worldâ) that slightly disrupt formal register.
Inconsistent footnote and bibliography formatting detracts from professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 4 fits the submissionâs strong clarity, accuracy and appropriate register. While language and style are consistently suitable, minor lapses in mechanics, formatting and occasional colloquialisms keep the work just below the highest level of precision and style consistency.

strandSelections.reasoning: Dialogue excerpts, timestamps, and secondary data are **mostly well embedded within sentences**, accompanied by interpretive commentary rather than left to stand alone.  Occasional formatting issues (superscripts, stray symbols) interrupt flow, and at times evidence could be more seamlessly blended, but integration is **generally smooth and effective**, matching band 4.

The student demonstrates **secure factual knowledge of the plot, characters and socio-historical context** of Parasite (e.g., the âgolden spoon vs. dirt spoonâ phenomenon, housing conditions in Seoul, Marxist terminology).  Discussion of each symbol (rock, basements, peaches, smell, rain) shows a **sustained link between film details and the overarching question of class disparity**, moving beyond surface description.  However, interpretation stops short of probing the directorâs more nuanced cinematic intentions (e.g., mise-en-scÃ¨ne, editing, tonal shifts) and occasionally lapses into thematic generalities (âforcing its audience to confront the realities of class oppressionâ).  The depth and originality therefore match the âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ descriptor.

The line of inquiry is stated early and each body paragraph tackles a discrete symbol, yielding a **logical progression** from introduction through conclusion.  Cohesion is mostly maintained with topic sentences and transitional phrases (âWhile the rock representsâ¦â, âFor the wealthy, the rainâ¦â).  Development is adequate, though some sections (peaches, smell) could be expanded for fuller balance.  Overall, organisation is **well managed and largely cohesive**, characteristic of band 4.

Quotations from dialogue and time-stamps are **consistently supplied** for each main point and are relevant to the symbols discussed (e.g., Mr Parkâs remarks on smell, Ki-Wooâs comments on the stone).  Secondary sources on Korean inequality are also cited.  These examples **clearly reinforce the candidateâs claims** and are mostly integrated into the prose.  The selection, while adequate, is not especially varied (few cinematic techniques or visual frames are referenced), preventing it from reaching the âwell-chosen and sophisticatedâ level of band 5.

The essay maintains a **formal, analytical register** appropriate to an HL essay.  Third-person voice, discipline-specific terminology, and cautious modality are sustained throughout.  Occasional colloquialisms (âdog-eat-dog worldâ) are present but do not dominate.  Therefore, register and style are **consistently suitable**, meriting band 4.

Language is **clear, academically appropriate and largely accurate**.  Sentence structures are varied, and vocabulary (e.g., âdehumanisation,â âbourgeoisieâ) is used correctly.  Minor mechanical errors and formatting artefacts (rogue symbols, inconsistent citation style) appear but rarely impede comprehension.  Thus, accuracy is good rather than flawless, fitting band 4.

The essay offers **generally appropriate analysis** of how each symbol functions within a Marxist reading.  Assertions such as the rock embodying âfalse consciousnessâ or rain exposing âdisproportionate sufferingâ show understanding of authorial purpose.  Yet much of the commentary remains explanatory or thematic; there is **limited close reading of cinematic choices** (lighting, camera angles, sound) and some ideas drift into plot summary (e.g., recounting the peach-allergy deception).  Insight flickers but is not sustained, aligning with band 3.

annotations.comment: The essayâs research question is clearly framed, focusing on symbolism and class critique. Consider adding an âIntroductionâ subheading to enhance structural clarity and guide the reader more effectively.

The introduction effectively contextualises the film within South Koreaâs socio-economic landscape, demonstrating strong factual knowledge of cultural phenomena like the âgolden spoon versus dirt spoon.â
The opening paragraph leans heavily on plot summary rather than advancing analytical insight; consider reducing narrative retelling to focus more on interpretive depth.
The clear statement of key symbols and the adoption of a Marxist framework early on provides a coherent roadmap for readers, showcasing purposeful organization of analytical direction.
This sentence fragment ending abruptly interrupts the flow; complete the thought and ensure smooth transitions between concepts to maintain cohesive argumentation.
The footnote formatting appears inconsistent with stray HTML artifacts (â<sup>â); streamline notation to adhere to academic citation conventions and improve readability.
The contextualisation of the suseok (landscape rock) within East Asian tradition enriches interpretation and demonstrates robust cultural understanding.
The paragraph devotes considerable space to narrating plot details; narrowing the focus to analytical insights will strengthen the evaluation of how the rock functions symbolically.
Expand your analysis of the semi-basement and hidden basement by discussing specific filmic choicesâsuch as lighting, shot composition, or sound designâto deepen evaluative insight.
The text ends mid-sentence (âThis basement also symbolizesâ); ensure complete sentences to maintain clarity and coherence in your argumentation.
Footnote citations display inconsistent style (varying use of superscripts and italics); adopt a single format (e.g., MLA or APA) for uniformity.
The linkage of Marxist terminology (e.g., bourgeoisie, proletariat) to the basement symbolism is conceptually strong and reflects sound theoretical understanding.
The phrase âdog-eat-dog worldâ injects a colloquial tone that clashes with the formal analytical register; replace it with discipline-specific language to maintain academic style.
Consider integrating lengthy quotations (e.g., Mr. Parkâs comments on smell) more succinctly or paraphrasing key segments to preserve narrative flow.

There is minimal reference to cinematic techniques (camera angles, sound design) when discussing the smell symbol; adding this will elevate the analysis from thematic description to filmic evaluation.
Footnote numbering resets inconsistently here; verify sequential ordering and apply consistent superscript formatting throughout to uphold scholarly norms.
The analysis of the rain motif effectively contrasts the lived experiences of different social classes, showcasing insightful interpretation of economic vulnerability.
Support the rain symbolism with specific scene details (e.g., shot duration, soundtrack) to reinforce your evaluative claims with concrete cinematic evidence.
The concluding paragraph largely restates points rather than synthesising insights or offering a final evaluative statement; aim to drive home original analytical conclusions.
Bibliography heading is clear, but ensure consistency in citation formatting style (e.g., indent second lines, uniform punctuation) across all entries.
This bibliography entry appears to conflate multiple sources in one block; separate entries clearly and alphabetically order them to adhere to academic standards.
Reference list entries mix different formatting conventions for italics and URLs; standardise to a single style manual (e.g., APA or MLA) for professional consistency.
Long URLs disrupt the visual flow; consider using DOI or hyperlink text where permissible to improve readability and adhere to academic style guides.
The page header contains OCR artefacts resembling a page number; remove these elements to maintain a clean academic document layout.
The use of reputable sources such as Reuters and Vox enhances the credibility of your secondary evidence, reflecting careful selection of supporting materials.
